description:
[NARROWED 2026-05-26 per TASK-081 plan-mode discovery — bundled scope split into TASK-085 (DEC-0001 impl, hard-blocked on FGAP-115), TASK-086 (DEC-0002 impl), TASK-087 (DEC-0003 impl, hard-blocked on FGAP-115). This task retains ONLY the structural-additive minimum: spec.tools threading from AgentSpec → CompiledAgent + parentGrant param on DispatchContext + child-grant ⊆ parent-grant clamp enforcement at executeAgent dispatch boundary per DEC-0047. NO model-resolution surface change (DEC-0003 = TASK-087). NO ExtensionContext currentModel resolution (DEC-0001 = TASK-085, blocked on FGAP-115). NO thinking-seam enforcement + warning trace (DEC-0002 = TASK-086). NO actual tool-injection into context.tools (spec.tools → real Tool object translation is FEAT-004/005 territory). The clamp is METADATA enforcement at dispatch boundary; it prevents spec-authoring violations (a sub-agent spec authored with tools beyond its grant gets refused at dispatch) regardless of whether the agent invokes tools at runtime.]

--- ORIGINAL DESCRIPTION (pre-narrowing) ---

Pi-jit-agents element of FEAT-001 — additive build inside pi-jit-agents only; no consumer touching. Wire spec.tools through the agent layer end-to-end: AgentSpec already carries `tools?: string[]` (types.ts:27, verified); extend compile.ts to thread that tools field into the compiled agent's grant; extend executeAgent (jit-runtime.ts:402) to accept a parent-grant param + enforce parent-grant ⊆ child-grant clamp at the dispatch boundary per DEC-0047 (operation-granular, default EMPTY, no inheritance of full defaults). Per the narrowed DEC-0044, this work happens in the pi-jit-agents library itself; the pi-agent-dispatch extension wraps this surface later for the sub-agent agent-as-tool pi.registerTool site (FEAT-004/005). Per DEC-0003 enacted (a71c782 / 7ca1a33): parseModelSpec moves into executeAgent boundary as an internal helper (not a fifth exported surface — four-boundary-surfaces declaration of jit-agents-spec §2 stands). Per DEC-0001 enacted (bb45880): bare model ids resolve against ExtensionContext at dispatch time; missing binding → explicit error. Per DEC-0002 enacted (bb1bc27): executeAgent enforces thinking=off when toolChoice.type='tool'; warning channel emits TraceEntry via trace-writer pipeline. NO pi-workflows touching. NO pi-behavior-monitors touching. NO consumer cascade. The duplicate AgentSpec/loader/compile in pi-workflows continues unchanged (deleted in TASK-082); monitor synthetic CompiledAgent build continues unchanged (replaced in TASK-083). FGAP-099 (clamp) subsumed here at the library boundary. Split from original TASK-080 (2026-05-26) per user direction: focus is pi-jit-agents element + POC first; consumer cascade follows.
acceptance_criteria: AgentSpec.tools (already declared at types.ts:27) threads through compile.ts into CompiledAgent.tools (new optional field) — verified gap: compile.ts currently does NOT read spec.tools
DispatchContext gains parentGrant?: string[] field (default-empty semantics per DEC-0047: undefined = empty set)

executeAgent enforces clamp BEFORE pi-ai complete() call at jit-runtime.ts:510: compute child-grant \ parent-grant; non-empty difference throws GrantViolationError (new error type) naming the offending tokens + the agent name

Engine unit tests added covering: clamp-passes-when-child-subset-of-parent + clamp-throws-when-child-not-subset + default-empty-parent-refuses-non-empty-child + GrantViolationError carries the offending tokens + agent name

NO surface changes beyond the additive fields named above: executeAgent signature retains existing pre-resolved Model<Api> + auth (no DEC-0003 surface change); no thinking forwarding (no DEC-0002 implementation); no ExtensionContext resolution (no DEC-0001 implementation)
NO consumer code touched: pi-workflows + pi-behavior-monitors source unchanged (those cascade in TASK-082/083 after POC)
pi-jit-agents engine unit tests green at every commit (husky); bundled-artifact tests not gates per DEC-0048

VALIDATION = real checks: build/check/test exit codes (no pipe-masking), runtime demo (tsx-eval direct invocation of executeAgent exercising clamp-pass + clamp-throw paths with concrete grant tokens), adversarial probe (fresh-context Explore agent re-greps: zero spec.tools access in jit-runtime.ts past clamp check; zero context.tools construction from spec.tools at this layer; clamp invoked before any LLM call)
notes: Split 2026-05-26 from TASK-080 per user direction (pi-jit-agents element first; then POC; then consumer cascade in 082/083). Authority: analysis/2026-05-26-jit-agents-user-intentions.jsonl (JI-010/021/023/024/027/028/029) + analysis/2026-05-26-decisions-substrate-revision-grounding.md (canon-mapping). External prior art: analysis/2026-05-26-pi-subagents-eval-vs-jit-intentions.md (grant mechanism = `pi --tools` at subprocess boundary; depth-clamp generalizes to tool-clamp; opt-in-from-empty default). No release. Narrowed 2026-05-26 per plan-mode exploration discovery. The four findings (AgentSpec.tools inert past load; ExtensionContext currentModel gap = FGAP-115; DEC-0003 surface-change scope; DEC-0002 TraceEntry schema extension needed) split the bundled scope: this task retains the additive minimum (spec.tools threading + clamp); the other three implementations become TASK-085 (DEC-0001, blocked on FGAP-115), TASK-086 (DEC-0002), TASK-087 (DEC-0003, blocked on FGAP-115). POC (TASK-084) needs ONLY this narrow scope to validate the context→jit architectural shape — full executeAgent canon implementation (DEC-0001/0002/0003) is downstream and not POC-gating.
